_comment: Butler design tokens - the machine-readable contract for the shared-tablet family concierge. The decision record (design/design-decision-record.md) explains WHY; this file is WHAT gets implemented on Expo / react-native-web. Values are density-independent px. Every fg/bg pair the design ships is listed in contrast_pairs so design_score.py can verify WCAG 2.1 AA in both themes. The decorative hairline `border` is intentionally low-contrast and is NOT a meaning-carrying boundary (surface already separates from bg), so it is exempt from the 3:1 rule and not declared as a contrast pair; input and other meaning-carrying boundaries use `border-strong`, which IS declared and passes 3:1. Established under spike #42; see Butler.KnowledgeBase/adr/ ADR-0001..0010.

brand.name: Butler
brand.voice: Calm concierge: understated, competent, gracious. Says 'Ready when you are.' and 'All caught up.', never 'You forgot!' and never gamified or naggy.

claim_colors._comment: Six mutually distinguishable person colors. Each is permanently paired with a glyph and always accompanied by the person's name, so the identity survives with color removed entirely (redundant encoding: color + glyph + name). Blue is a deliberate royal/sky blue kept visibly distinct from the teal `primary` so a glow never reads as the app accent. Light variants are dark enough to serve as a glyph/left-bar on the ivory surface at >= 3:1 and to carry `on-claim` white text as a filled chip; dark variants lighten for the warm-black surface and carry `on-claim` dark ink. Used primarily as accents (left color-bar, glyph, subtle tint wash) with body text kept on `surface`.

contrast_pairs.name: L claim berry glyph on surface
L claim marigold glyph on surface
L claim grass glyph on surface
L claim blue glyph on surface
L claim violet glyph on surface
L claim clay glyph on surface
D claim berry glyph on surface
D claim marigold glyph on surface
D claim grass glyph on surface
D claim blue glyph on surface
D claim violet glyph on surface
D claim clay glyph on surface

type_scale_note: 1.25 modular scale with an 18px body floor. Adjacent steps are close to but not all exactly 1.25 (14, 18, 22, 28 are single steps); display 44 sits two steps above title 28 (28 -> 35 -> 44), skipping the ~35 step on purpose for a stronger hero size. So do not read every adjacent pair as an exact 1.25 ratio.

type_family.sans: Inter, 'Nunito Sans', system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif
type_family._note: One warm humanist sans (Inter / Nunito Sans character), weights 400/600/700, line-height ~1.4 for body. On this stack the family is a role: if a bundled font is not present, fall back to the system humanist sans in the stack above at zero cost.

focus._comment: Cross-platform visible focus (ADR-0010). WCAG 2.5 focus ring at >= 3:1. On web this renders as an outline; on RN/native there is no :focus-visible pseudo-class, so the ring is drawn as a real bordered ring (a View border or a wrapping ring layer) toggled on focus state, wired via accessibilityState. The visible-focus requirement therefore has a concrete cross-platform realization, not only a web pseudo-class.
focus.native-render: a 3px border-ring at 2px offset drawn around the focused element on focus, not the web :focus-visible pseudo-class

density._comment: Two contexts, one system (ADR-0008). The hub is the ambient config read across a room; organizer flows are the focused config on a phone/portrait. Same palette, type, and components; two densities. secondary-size is the assignee/who and status text: at hub density it steps up from caption 14 to body 18 so the 'who' stays legible at room distance, while the focused density keeps caption 14.

motion._comment: Gentle and quick; always gated on prefers-reduced-motion (ADR-0006).

a11y.label: a visible bound label, never placeholder-as-label

card._note: border here is the decorative hairline; the surface fill is what separates the card from bg.

neutral.claim-cue: small glyph + thin claim-color underbar, calm

active.claim-tint-wash: claim light/dark at low alpha over surface

a11y.label: person name
a11y.note: identity carried by color AND glyph AND name

active-owner.state: glow: claim left-bar + glyph + slight elevation

inactive-owner.state: recede via a DECLARED muted treatment: text at text-muted and lighter weight, NOT blanket opacity; every fg/bg stays a declared contrast pair passing AA, so a receded row is still legible in the glance

queued.note: offline write, resolves to done on sync

failed.note: optimistic completion that did not go through: icon AND text AND color (danger) with a retry; copy 'That did not go through. Tap to try again.'

a11y.note: done shown by check icon AND muted text, never color alone; failed shown by icon AND text AND color

offline-chip._note: quiet and muted, never an alarming red banner; the board stays fully usable behind it. At hub density the text steps up to body 18 so it is legible at room distance (see density.secondary-size).

balance-bar-segment.order: stable household/roster order, NOT sorted by share, so the bar never reads as a ranking
balance-bar-segment._note: one horizontal proportional bar; each person's completed share is a segment in their claim color + glyph, laid out in stable household order; framed as trending toward even; no rank, no numbers-first (exact percentages are not foregrounded on the segments), no leaderboard

a11y.label: Balance this week: trending toward even. Each person's share of chores done, in household order: <names in stable order>. No ranking language.
